cards.prompt: Three parts: source, query, execution — where filter keeps even numbers (0 included)
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq; int[] numbers = [0,1,2,3,4,5,6]; var numQuery = from num in numbers where (num % 2) == 0 select num; Console.WriteLine(string.Join(" ", numQuery));

cards.prompt: Query variable holds no data: source mutated before iteration, new items appear
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq; using System.Collections.Generic; var list = new List<int>{1,2}; var q = from x in list where x > 1 select x; list.Add(5); list.Add(9); Console.WriteLine(string.Join(",", q));

cards.prompt: Same query executed twice around a source mutation reflects the change (2 then 4)
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq; using System.Collections.Generic; var src = new List<int>{2,4}; var q = from x in src where x % 2 == 0 select x; int a = q.Count(); src.Add(6); src.Add(8); int b = q.Count(); Console.WriteLine($"{a} {b}");
